title: Bletilla formosana
taxonomy: Bletilla formosana is a species of orchid native to Taiwan. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is classified as a type of terrestrial orchid. Bletilla formosana is also known as the 'Formosa Bletilla' or 'Taiwan Bletilla'. This species is often confused with other Bletilla species, but it can be distinguished by its distinctive growth habit and flower color. Bletilla formosana is an evergreen terrestrial orchid with strap-like leaves and white, pink, or purple flowers.
uses: Bletilla formosana has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Taiwan, for its potential health benefits. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, Bletilla formosana may also have antimicrobial properties, making it potentially useful in the treatment of certain infections. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential applications. Its traditional use suggests that it may have a range of health benefits, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.

parts: Bletilla formosana. Bletilla formosana, also known as the Formosa orchid, is a species of flowering plant.
